title: atriplex patula subsp. verreauxii
intro: Atriplex patula subsp. verreauxii, also known as the Saltbush, is a type of shrub or small tree that belongs to the Amaranthaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Atriplex patula subsp. verreauxii include treating wounds, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or applied topically as a poultice to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
